title: Teams Train for March 26 Semifinals — Italy, Kosovo, Sweden Hold Final Sessions
detail: The final preparation day before the March 26 UEFA playoff semifinals saw all competing nations hold press conferences and training sessions. Italy's Roberto Mancini, under enormous pressure to avoid a third consecutive World Cup absence after missing 2018 and 2022, confirmed Sandro Tonali would start. Sweden's manager confirmed Viktor Gyökeres fit and available despite prior fitness speculation, a critical boost ahead of the Ukraine clash. Kosovo's Alban Hajdari stated his side were 'ready to make history' against Slovakia. For the intercontinental bracket, both Bolivia and Jamaica trained at Estadio BBVA and Estadio Akron respectively, with FIFA confirming the venues were match-ready. Bosnia-Herzegovina's Edin Džeko — at 40 one of the oldest players in playoff contention — was confirmed fit to lead the squad.

title: Iraq National Team Arrives in Monterrey via Amman Charter — Logistics Battle Before March 31 Final
detail: Iraq's national football team arrived in Monterrey on March 25 via a chartered flight from Amman, Jordan, after traveling overland from Baghdad to avoid airspace disrupted by the ongoing Iran-US-Israel regional conflict. Iraq had traveled from Baghdad to Jordan on March 21 after direct flights to Mexico from Baghdad were unavailable. The intercontinental bypass through Amman added approximately 36 hours to the team's travel schedule ahead of the March 31 final against Bolivia at Estadio BBVA. Iraq coach Radhi Shenaishil called the travel arrangements 'a testament to the team's dedication' and confirmed all squad members were fit after a rest period in Amman.
